debug_info.goal:
d : Type u_1
inst✝² : Fintype d
inst✝¹ : DecidableEq d
𝕜 : Type u_5
inst✝ : RCLike 𝕜
A B : Matrix d d 𝕜
hA : A.IsHermitian
hB : B.IsHermitian
hAB : Commute A B
j : d
⊢ A.mulVec ((@Matrix.sharedEigenbasis d inst✝² inst✝¹ 𝕜 inst✝ A B hA hB hAB) j) =
    @Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA d inst✝² inst✝¹ 𝕜 inst✝ A B hA hB hAB j •
      WithLp.ofLp ((@Matrix.sharedEigenbasis d inst✝² inst✝¹ 𝕜 inst✝ A B hA hB hAB) j)

failed_attempts:
classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from the decomposition into the joint eigenspaces, so each basis
  -- vector lies in an intersection of eigenspaces and hence is an eigenvector of `A`.
  rcases (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).mem_subordinate? j with ⟨μ, hμ⟩
  -- `μ` is a pair of eigenvalues; the first component is the eigenvalue for `A`.
  have hAμ : A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) = (μ.1 : 𝕜) • (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hμ).1
  -- Convert the statement from linear maps on Euclidean space to matrices acting by `mulVec`,
  -- and identify the scalar as `sharedEigenvalueA`.
  have hAμ' :
      A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (μ.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    ext i
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, PiLp.ofLp_apply] using
      congrArg (fun v => v i) hAμ
  have h_shared :
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = (μ.1 : 𝕜).re := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- use the eigenvector equation to compute the Rayleigh quotient
    have :
        dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
          (μ.1 : 𝕜) * dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) := by
      simpa [hAμ', dotProduct_smul_right] using rfl
    -- inner product with itself is 1 since basis is orthonormal
    have hnorm :
        dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) = (1 : 𝕜) := by
      simpa [dotProduct, WithLp.ofLp_apply] using
        (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).orthonormal j j
    -- conclude real part equals eigenvalue's real part
    simp [this, hnorm, mul_one]
  -- finish: replace eigenvalue by the defined `sharedEigenvalueA`
  have : A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
      ((sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j : 𝕜) •
        WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- since `A` is Hermitian, the eigenvalue is real
    have hμ_real : (μ.1 : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (μ.1 : 𝕜).re := by
      -- eigenvalues of a Hermitian operator are real
      exact (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA).eigenvalue_eq_of_mem_eigenspace_real
        (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hμ).1
    -- rewrite `hAμ'` using the realness and `h_shared`
    simpa [h_shared, RCLike.ofReal_eq_coe, hμ_real] using hAμ'
  -- switch from `WithLp.ofLp` to the stated form
  simpa using this

simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using
    (hA.mulVec_eq_smul_of_isHermitian_of_inner_eq (v := sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) rfl)

classical
  -- Use that the shared eigenbasis is subordinate to the joint eigenspace decomposition.
  -- Hence each basis vector is in an eigenspace of `A`, so it is an eigenvector.
  have hmem :
      sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈
        ⨆ (μ₁₂ : Eigenvalues A.toEuclideanLin × Eigenvalues B.toEuclideanLin),
          (eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 ⊓ eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.2) := by
    simp
  -- Pick the component (μ₁₂) whose summand contains the basis vector.
  rcases Submodule.mem_iSup.1 (by simpa using hmem) with ⟨μ₁₂, hμ⟩
  have hμA : sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈ eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 := (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hμ).1
  -- Turn eigenspace membership into an eigenvector equation.
  have hev :
      A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hμA
  -- Move back to matrices and identify the eigenvalue with `sharedEigenvalueA`.
  -- Since the vector is normalized, the eigenvalue equals `re ⟪v, Av⟫`.
  have hnorm : ‖sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j‖ = (1 : ℝ) := by
    simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq j
  have hinner :
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = RCLike.re (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- `A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v = WithLp.ofLp (A.toEuclideanLin v)`
    -- and `A.toEuclideanLin v = μ • v`
    -- so `⟪v, A v⟫ = μ * ⟪v, v⟫ = μ`.
    have : dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
        (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) = (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) := by
      -- rewrite `A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v` as `WithLp.ofLp (A.toEuclideanLin v)`
      -- then use `hev` and `‖v‖=1`.
      have hmul :
          A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
            WithLp.ofLp (A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) := by
        ext i
        simp [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct]
      -- use `hev`
      rw [hmul, hev]
      -- dotProduct with scalar multiple
      simp [dotProduct_smul_right, hnorm]
    simpa [this]
  -- conclude
  -- rewrite `A.mulVec` on basis vector using `hev`
  -- and convert scalar using `hinner`.
  have hmul' :
      A *ᵥ (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- transport `hev` to matrices
    ext i
    -- `A.mulVec v = (A.toEuclideanLin v)` pointwise
    -- and `WithLp.ofLp` is definitional
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using congrArg (fun x => x i) (by
      simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply] using hev)
  -- replace eigenvalue with the defined real one
  -- note `μ₁₂.1` is real since `A` is Hermitian
  -- so `(μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (re μ₁₂.1)`
  have hμ_real : (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (RCLike.re (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜)) := by
    -- eigenvalues of a Hermitian operator are real
    simpa using (hA.toEuclideanLin.isSymmetric.isReal_eigenvalue μ₁₂.1)
  -- final rewrite
  calc
    A *ᵥ (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
        = (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := hmul'
    _ = (RCLike.ofReal ((sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j)) •
          WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
          simp [hμ_real, hinner]
    _ = (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j •
          WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
          simp [RCLike.real_smul_eq_coe_mul]

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is a common eigenbasis; use the corresponding `mulVec` lemma for the underlying construction.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, Matrix.sharedEigenvectorUnitary,
    Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvectorUnitary_mulVec] using
    (hA.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis (j := j))

classical
  -- each vector of the shared basis lies in some joint eigenspace, hence is an eigenvector of `A`
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA] using
    (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  -- use that the shared eigenbasis is subordinate to the joint-eigenspace decomposition
  -- hence each basis vector is an eigenvector for `A`
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.sharedEigenbasis] using
    (LinearMap.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` comes from the direct-sum decomposition into intersections of eigenspaces,
  -- so each basis vector lies in an eigenspace of `A`.
  rcases (LinearMap.mem_iSup_eigenspace_inf_eigenspace_of_isSymmetric_of_isSymmetric_of_commute
    (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
    (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
    (commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)
    (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) with ⟨μ₁₂, hμ⟩
  have hmemA : WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) ∈ eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 := by
    exact (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hμ).1
  have hvec : A.toEuclideanLin (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
      μ₁₂.1 • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hmemA
  have hmulVec : A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
      μ₁₂.1 • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply] using hvec
  -- identify the eigenvalue via the definition of `sharedEigenvalueA`
  have hμreal : (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = μ₁₂.1.re := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- use `hmulVec` and orthonormality to collapse the dot product
    simpa [hmulVec, dotProduct_smul_right, inner_self_eq_one] 
  -- finally rewrite scalar as a real scalar (Hermitian eigenvalues are real)
  have hμim : μ₁₂.1.im = 0 := by
    -- eigenvalues of Hermitian are real
    simpa using (hA.im_eq_zero_of_mem_eigenspace hmemA)
  -- convert `μ₁₂.1` to `RCLike.ofReal μ₁₂.1.re` using `hμim`
  have hμcoe : μ₁₂.1 = RCLike.ofReal μ₁₂.1.re := by
    ext <;> simp [RCLike.ofReal, hμim]
  -- now finish
  have : A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
      RCLike.ofReal ((sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j) •
        WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- rewrite `μ₁₂.1` in `hmulVec`
    simpa [hμreal, hμcoe] using hmulVec
  simpa [WithLp.ofLp] using this

classical
  -- view `sharedEigenbasis j` as a vector in `EuclideanSpace`
  have hmem :
      (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d) ∈
        (LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin (LinearMap.Eigenvalues.mk _ ?_) ⊓
            LinearMap.eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin (LinearMap.Eigenvalues.mk _ ?_)) := by
    simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j).2
  -- extract the eigenvector property for `A`
  have hAeig :
      A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d) =
        (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB j : 𝕜) •
          (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d) := by
    have := (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hmem).1
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using this
  -- rewrite in terms of `mulVec` and `WithLp.ofLp`
  ext i
  simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, WithLp.ofLp_apply] using congrArg (fun v => v i) hAeig

classical
  -- use that the shared basis vector lies in the (finite) direct sum of simultaneous eigenspaces,
  -- hence in some intersection eigenspace, so it is an eigenvector of `A`
  have hSymmA : A.toEuclideanLin.IsSymmetric := (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
  have hSymmB : B.toEuclideanLin.IsSymmetric := (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
  set φ := (LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis (d := d) (𝕜 := 𝕜) (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
    hSymmA hSymmB (commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)) with hφ
  have hφeq : φ = sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB := by
    simp [Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, hφ]
  subst hφeq
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is subordinate to the decomposition into simultaneous eigenspaces
  -- so each basis vector is a simultaneous eigenvector; extract eigenvalue from dot product
  have hv_mem :
      WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) ∈
        ⨆ (μ₁₂ : Eigenvalues A.toEuclideanLin × Eigenvalues B.toEuclideanLin),
          (LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 ⊓ LinearMap.eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.2) := by
    -- follows since the decomposition is `⊤`
    have htop :=
      (Commute.isSymmetric_directSumDecomposition (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
        hSymmA hSymmB (commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)).iSup_eq_top
    -- rewrite membership in `⊤`
    simpa [htop]
  -- pick one component containing the vector
  rcases Submodule.mem_iSup.1 hv_mem with ⟨μ₁₂, hμ⟩
  have hμA : WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) ∈ LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 := by
    exact (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hμ).1
  -- eigenvector equation in `EuclideanSpace`
  have hEig :
      A.toEuclideanLin (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
        (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hμA
  -- translate back to `mulVec`
  have hMulVec :
      A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply] using hEig
  -- compute the chosen eigenvalue equals the dot product definition
  have hLam :
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = RCLike.re (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- use orthonormality to extract scalar from inner product
    have hinner :
        dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
          (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) := by
      -- `dotProduct` is `inner` on `EuclideanSpace`; the basis vector has norm 1
      -- and is orthonormal with itself
      have hone : ‖(sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d)‖ = 1 := by
        simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq (i := j)
      have hinner1 : ⟪(sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d),
          (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d)⟫_𝕜 = 1 := by
        simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).inner_self_eq_one (i := j)
      -- use eigenvector equation
      have : (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
          (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := hMulVec
      -- unfold `dotProduct` and simplify with orthonormality
      simp [dotProduct, this, inner_smul_right, hinner1]
    simpa [hinner]
  -- finally, rewrite scalar as real part (since `A` is Hermitian, eigenvalue is real)
  -- but we only need equality of scalar actions in `𝕜`; use `RCLike.ofReal` coercion
  have hμreal : (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- `μ₁₂.1` is an eigenvalue of a symmetric map, hence real
    have : IsROrC.re (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j := by
      simpa [hLam] using (Eq.symm hLam)
    -- turn `re` equality into value equality via `ofReal_re`
    -- for `RCLike`, `ofReal (re z)` is `z` when `im z = 0`; use symmetry to get `im=0`
    have him : RCLike.im (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = 0 := by
      -- eigenvalues of symmetric maps are real
      exact (hSymmA.im_eigenvalue_eq_zero μ₁₂.1)
    ext <;> simp [this, him]
  -- conclude
  -- note: `mulVec` on `EuclideanSpace` uses `WithLp.ofLp`; remove it on the left
  simpa [Matrix.mulVec, hμreal, PiLp.smul_apply, Pi.smul_apply] using hMulVec

classical
  have hj : A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j • (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [sharedEigenvalueA] using
      (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.apply_orthonormalBasis_eq_smul
        (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB)
        (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j))
  ext i
  simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, PiLp.smul_apply, Pi.smul_apply] using
    congrArg (fun v => v i) hj

classical
  -- use the direct-sum decomposition into joint eigenspaces; each basis vector lies in one summand
  have hA' : A.toEuclideanLin.IsSymmetric := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA
  have hB' : B.toEuclideanLin.IsSymmetric := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB
  have hAB' : Commute A.toEuclideanLin B.toEuclideanLin := commute_euclideanLin hAB
  let dec :=
    Commute.isSymmetric_directSumDecomposition (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin) hA' hB' hAB'
  -- the shared basis comes from a subordinate orthonormal basis to `dec`; hence each vector lies in some joint eigenspace
  -- so it is an eigenvector for `A`
  -- package this as an existence statement and then compute the eigenvalue by the defining inner-product formula
  rcases (show ∃ (μ₁₂ : Eigenvalues A.toEuclideanLin × Eigenvalues B.toEuclideanLin),
      (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) ∈
        (eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 ⊓ eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.2) from by
      -- membership follows from construction via `subordinateOrthonormalBasis` and reindexing
      -- `simp` can unfold `sharedEigenbasis` and discharge the membership goal.
      simp [Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, dec]) with ⟨μ₁₂, hj⟩
  have hjA : (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) ∈ eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 :=
    (by simpa [Submodule.mem_inf] using hj).1
  rcases hjA with ⟨hjA⟩
  -- `hjA` is the eigenvector equation in the linear-map language; translate to matrix `mulVec`
  have hmul :
      A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (RCLike.ofReal μ₁₂.1.1) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_mulVec, Matrix.IsHermitian, LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hjA
  -- identify the real eigenvalue with `sharedEigenvalueA` by taking inner products with the unit vector
  have hn : ‖(WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d)‖ = 1 := by
    simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq_one j
  have hreal :
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = μ₁₂.1.1 := by
    -- take dot product with the basis vector and use `hmul`
    have :
        sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB j =
          RCLike.re (dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            ((RCLike.ofReal μ₁₂.1.1) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j))) := by
      simp [Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA, hmul]
    -- simplify using orthonormality: dotProduct v (a • v) = a * dotProduct v v, and dotProduct v v = 1
    -- and `re (ofReal r) = r`.
    simpa [dotProduct_smul, dotProduct_smul_right, hn, mul_one] using this
  -- finish by rewriting `hmul` with `sharedEigenvalueA`
  -- and dropping the `ofReal` since scalar on `EuclideanSpace` is `𝕜` but `sharedEigenvalueA` is `ℝ`
  -- via `RCLike.ofReal`.
  -- The statement is in `Matrix.mulVec` on `d → 𝕜`, so coe back.
  have :
      A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (RCLike.ofReal ((sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j)) •
          WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [hreal] using hmul
  -- coe to the `d → 𝕜` view
  simpa [PiLp.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul] using this

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` comes from the simultaneous diagonalization construction, so each basis vector
  -- lies in some `eigenspace A μ₁ ∩ eigenspace B μ₂`. Unfolding the construction and using that
  -- membership gives the eigenvector equation for `A`.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis,
    Matrix.sharedEigenvectorUnitary, Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA] using
    (Matrix.IsHermitian.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis (A := A) hA j)

classical
  -- use that `sharedEigenbasis` is a shared eigenbasis coming from the simultaneous diagonalization
  -- construction; hence each basis vector is an eigenvector for `A`
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA] using
    (Matrix.IsHermitian.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis
      (A := A) (hA := hA) (i := j))

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is a simultaneous eigenbasis coming from the joint eigenspace decomposition,
  -- hence each basis vector is an eigenvector of `A`.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA]
    using (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built subordinate to the joint eigenspace decomposition, so each basis vector
  -- lies in an intersection of eigenspaces; hence it is an eigenvector of `A`.
  rcases (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).subtype_mem (i := j) with hj
  -- unpack membership in the intersection, then in the eigenspace
  have hjA : sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈ LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin
      (⟨_, (show Module.End.HasEigenvalue A.toEuclideanLin _ from by
        simpa using (Submodule.ne_bot_iff.1 (by
          classical
          simpa using (Submodule.ne_bot_of_mem (show sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈
            (LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin ?_ ⊓ LinearMap.eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin ?_ ) from hj) )) )⟩ : Eigenvalues A.toEuclideanLin) := by
    simpa [Submodule.mem_inf] using hj.1
  -- use the defining property of eigenspace membership
  rcases hjA with ⟨hjAeq⟩
  -- convert between `toEuclideanLin` and `mulVec`
  ext i
  simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, sharedEigenvalueA, dotProduct, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.dotProduct]
    using congr_fun hjAeq i

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from the joint-eigenspace decomposition, hence each basis
  -- vector lies in an intersection of eigenspaces for `A` and `B`.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using
    (Matrix.IsHermitian.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis (A := A) hA j)

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is obtained by reindexing a subordinate orthonormal basis coming from the
  -- joint eigenspace decomposition; so each vector lies in some `eigenspace A μ`.
  rcases (LinearMap.mem_iSup_eigenspace_inf_eigenspace_of_isSymmetric_of_commute
    (E := EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d)
    (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
    (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
    (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
    (commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)
    (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
    (by simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).mem_of_orthonormal j)) with
    ⟨μ₁₂, hμ₁₂⟩
  have hmemA : sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈ LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 := by
    have : sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈
        (LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.1 ⊓ LinearMap.eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin μ₁₂.2) := hμ₁₂
    exact this.1
  have hmul :
      A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hmemA
  have hreal : (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = RCLike.re (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- use the eigenvector equation and orthonormality
    have hinner :
        dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
          (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) := by
      -- convert to inner product form
      have hmul' :
          (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
            (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
        -- `mulVec` agrees with `toEuclideanLin`
        ext i
        simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using congrArg (fun v => v i) hmul
      -- now compute dot product with itself
      simpa [hmul', dotProduct_smul_right, (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).orthonormal.1 j j rfl,
        one_smul] 
    simpa [hinner]
  -- finish by rewriting the scalar as the real part and using `RCLike.real_smul_eq_coe_mul`
  have hscalar :
      (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- `A` is Hermitian, so eigenvalues are real
    have hμreal : RCLike.im (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = 0 := by
      -- standard fact: eigenvalues of symmetric/Hermitian are real
      simpa using (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA).im_eigenvalue_eq_zero hmemA
    have : (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (RCLike.re (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜)) := by
      exact (RCLike.ext (by simp) (by simpa [hμreal]))
    simp [this, hreal]
  -- convert `hmul` back to `mulVec`
  ext i
  have hmulVec :
      (A *ᵥ (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) i =
        (μ₁₂.1 : 𝕜) * (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) i := by
    simpa [Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using congrArg (fun v => v i) hmul
  -- apply the scalar rewrite
  simpa [PiLp.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Pi.smul_apply] using congrArg (fun v => v i) (by
    simpa [WithLp.ofLp_apply] using (congrArg (fun v => v i) (hscalar)))

classical
  -- work in the Euclidean space representation where the shared eigenbasis comes from
  let A' : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d →ₗ[𝕜] EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d := A.toEuclideanLin
  let B' : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d →ₗ[𝕜] EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d := B.toEuclideanLin
  have hA' : A'.IsSymmetric := (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
  have hB' : B'.IsSymmetric := (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
  have hAB' : Commute A' B' := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB
  -- abbreviate the basis vector
  set v : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d := sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j with hv
  -- v lies in some joint eigenspace component of the decomposition; extract eigenvalues
  have hmem :
      ∃ (μ : Eigenvalues A') (ν : Eigenvalues B'),
        v ∈ (eigenspace A' μ ⊓ eigenspace B' ν) := by
    -- `sharedEigenbasis` is a reindexing of a subordinate orthonormal basis for the joint decomposition,
    -- hence each basis vector belongs to one summand.
    classical
    -- unfold and use the `subordinateOrthonormalBasis` property
    simp [Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis] at hv ⊢
  rcases hmem with ⟨μ, ν, hvμν⟩
  have hvμ : v ∈ eigenspace A' μ := (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hvμν).1
  have hAeig : A' v = (μ : 𝕜) • v := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hvμ
  -- convert back to matrices / mulVec, and identify the scalar via inner product
  have hmul : A *ᵥ v = (μ : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp v := by
    -- `A.mulVec` corresponds to `A.toEuclideanLin` action
    ext i
    simpa [A', Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using congrArg (fun w => w i) hAeig
  have hμre : (μ : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (RCLike.re (dotProduct v (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v))) := by
    -- take inner product with v and use orthonormality (‖v‖ = 1)
    have hvnorm : ‖v‖ = 1 := by
      simpa [hv] using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq_one j
    have hinner :
        dotProduct v (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v) = μ := by
      -- from eigenvector equation and `dotProduct v v = 1`
      have : dotProduct v (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v) = dotProduct v ((μ : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp v) := by
        -- rewrite using matrix action equality on v
        have := congrArg (fun w => dotProduct v w) (by
          -- relate `A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v` to `A *ᵥ v`
          ext i; simp [WithLp.ofLp_apply, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct])
        simpa using this
      -- simplify RHS
      -- `dotProduct v (μ • v) = μ * dotProduct v v = μ`
      -- and `dotProduct v v = 1` from `hvnorm`
      have hvv : dotProduct v v = 1 := by
        simpa [dotProduct_self_eq_norm_sq, hvnorm] using (by
          have : (‖v‖ : 𝕜) = (1 : 𝕜) := by
            simpa [hvnorm] using (show (RCLike.ofReal ‖v‖ : 𝕜) = (1 : 𝕜) by simp [hvnorm])
          simp [dotProduct_self_eq_norm_sq, hvnorm])
      simpa [dotProduct_smul_right, hvv, mul_one] using this
    -- since μ is real (eigenvalues of symmetric map), reconstruct it from its real part
    -- `μ` is in `Eigenvalues A'`, hence coerces to 𝕜 with real part equal to itself
    simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, A', hv, hinner] 
  -- finish
  -- rewrite scalar to match definition of `sharedEigenvalueA`
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, hv, hμre] using hmul

classical
  -- the shared basis is built out of the joint eigenspace decomposition, so each basis vector
  -- lies in some `eigenspace A μ₁` and hence is an eigenvector of `A`
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis,
    Matrix.sharedEigenvectorUnitary, Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA]
    using (Matrix.IsHermitian.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis (A := A) hA j)

classical
  -- use the defining property of the shared eigenbasis coming from the direct sum decomposition
  have hcomm : Commute A.toEuclideanLin B.toEuclideanLin := Matrix.commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB
  -- the shared eigenbasis is constructed from simultaneous eigenspaces, hence each basis vector is an eigenvector
  -- for `A.toEuclideanLin`; translate back to `mulVec`.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.toEuclideanLin_mulVec, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using
    (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := hcomm) j)

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from `LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis`, which is a subordinate orthonormal
  -- basis of the joint eigenspace decomposition, hence each basis vector is an eigenvector of `A`.
  simpa [Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, sharedEigenvalueA] using
    (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  -- Let `v` be the shared eigenbasis vector.
  set v : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d := sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j
  -- `v` lies in the intersection of an eigenspace of `A.toEuclideanLin` and an eigenspace of `B.toEuclideanLin`.
  rcases (LinearMap.mem_isSymmetric_subordinateOrthonormalBasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j) with ⟨μ, hμ, hvμ⟩
  -- Convert the eigenvector statement back to matrices and define the eigenvalue by the dot product.
  have hv :
      A *ᵥ v = (RCLike.ofReal (RCLike.re (dotProduct v (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp v)))) • WithLp.ofLp v := by
    -- `hvμ` gives `A.toEuclideanLin v = μ • v`; translate and rewrite `μ` using `re` via Hermitian property.
    have hvμ' : A *ᵥ v = (μ : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp v := by
      simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, v] using congrArg WithLp.ofLp hvμ
    -- For Hermitian `A`, `μ` is real; replace by `ofReal (re μ)`.
    have hμreal : (μ : 𝕜) = RCLike.ofReal (RCLike.re (μ : 𝕜)) := by
      simpa using (RCLike.ofReal_re (μ : 𝕜)).symm
    simpa [hvμ', hμreal]
  -- Now unfold `sharedEigenvalueA` and simplify.
  subst v
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.mulVec] using hv

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is a simultaneous eigenbasis coming from the joint eigenspace decomposition.
  -- The corresponding statement is already available for `LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis`.
  simpa [Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis,
    Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, Matrix.commute_euclideanLin] using
    (LinearMap.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  have hj : A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [sharedEigenvalueA] using
      (Matrix.IsHermitian.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis (A := A) hA
        (i := (sharedEigenvectorUnitary hA hB hAB)⁻¹ *ᵥ (Pi.single j 1)))
  simpa using hj

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from joint eigenspaces of `A.toEuclideanLin` and `B.toEuclideanLin`,
  -- so each basis vector is an eigenvector of `A.toEuclideanLin`.
  have hj :
      ∃ (r : ℝ),
        A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
          (RCLike.ofReal r) • (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- Use that the basis is subordinate to the joint eigenspace decomposition.
    -- The index `j` corresponds to some joint eigenspace with eigenvalues in `Eigenvalues A × Eigenvalues B`.
    -- Hence the `A`-eigenvalue is real and gives the required scalar.
    simpa [Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, Commute.isSymmetric_directSumDecomposition,
      LinearMap.IsSymmetric.directSum_isInternal_of_commute', LinearMap.IsSymmetric.orthogonalFamily_eigenspace_inf_eigenspace']
      using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).toBasis.mem_eigenspace_toEuclideanLin (A := A.toEuclideanLin) j
  rcases hj with ⟨r, hr⟩
  -- Convert from the linear map statement back to `mulVec`.
  have hr' :
      A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (RCLike.ofReal r) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_mulVec, Matrix.mulVec, PiLp.ofLp_apply] using congrArg WithLp.ofLp hr
  -- Identify `r` with the definition of `sharedEigenvalueA`.
  have hr_eq :
      r = sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB j := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- Take inner product with the unit vector.
    have hnorm : ‖(sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d)‖ = 1 := by
      simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq_one j
    have hin :
        inner 𝕜 (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
          RCLike.ofReal r := by
      -- From the eigenvector relation.
      simpa [hr', inner_smul_right, hnorm, mul_one] using congrArg (fun x =>
        inner 𝕜 (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) x) hr'
    -- Take real parts.
    have := congrArg RCLike.re hin
    simpa using this.symm
  -- Finish.
  subst hr_eq
  simpa [hr', RCLike.real_smul_eq_coe_mul]

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from the joint eigenspace decomposition, so each basis vector is
  -- a common eigenvector of `A` and `B`.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct] using
    (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.mul_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  have hshared :=
    (Matrix.commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB).isSymmetric.sharedEigenbasis
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := Matrix.commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)
  have hjmem :
      sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈
        eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin (hshared.1 j) ⊓ eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin (hshared.2 j) := by
    simpa [LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, hshared] using
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
        |>.directSum_isInternal_of_commute'
          (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
          (hAB := Matrix.commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)
        |>.subordinateOrthonormalBasis_mem (x := j)
  have hAeig :
      A.toEuclideanLin (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
        (hshared.1 j : 𝕜) • (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    have : sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j ∈ eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin (hshared.1 j) := by
      exact (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hjmem).1
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using this
  have hreal :
      (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = hshared.1 j := by
    have hinner :
        (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j =
          RCLike.re (inner 𝕜 (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            ((hshared.1 j : 𝕜) • sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) := by
      simp [SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.toEuclideanLin, hAeig]
    simpa [hinner, inner_smul_right, OrthonormalBasis.inner_right_self, RCLike.mul_re,
      RCLike.re_ofReal, one_mul] using rfl
  ext i
  simpa [Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.toEuclideanLin, hreal, hAeig, Pi.smul_apply]

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is obtained from the subordinate orthonormal basis coming from the
  -- joint-eigenspace decomposition, hence each basis vector lies in some intersection
  -- `eigenspace A μ₁ ⊓ eigenspace B μ₂`, so in particular is an eigenvector of `A`.
  simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, inner_smul_left, real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq, mulVec] using
    (LinearMap.IsSymmetric.mulVec_sharedEigenbasis
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (hA := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA)
      (hB := isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (hAB := commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j)

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from the orthogonal direct sum decomposition into
  -- `eigenspace A μ₁ ⊓ eigenspace B μ₂`, hence each basis vector is an eigenvector of `A`.
  rcases (LinearMap.mem_iSup_eigenspace_inf_eigenspace_iff_exists_eigenvalue
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB)
      (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) with ⟨μ₁, μ₂, hμ₁, hμ₂, hj⟩
  have hjA : A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) =
      (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    -- `hj` gives membership in the intersection of eigenspaces; extract the `A`-eigenvector equation.
    have hj' : WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) ∈
        (LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) ⊓
          LinearMap.eigenspace B.toEuclideanLin (RCLike.ofReal μ₂)) := by
      simpa using hj
    have hjA' : WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) ∈
        LinearMap.eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) := by
      exact (Submodule.mem_inf.1 hj').1
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hjA'
  have hnorm : ‖(sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)‖ = 1 := by
    simpa using (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq (i := j)
  have hdot : dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
      (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) = (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) := by
    -- Take dot product with the unit vector.
    calc
      dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
          (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j))
          =
          dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            ((RCLike.ofReal μ₁) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) := by
          simpa [hjA]
      _ =
          (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) * dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
            (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) := by
          simp [dotProduct_smul_right]
      _ = (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) := by
          have : dotProduct (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)
              (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) = 1 := by
            -- For orthonormal basis vectors, dotProduct with itself is 1.
            simpa [dotProduct, inner_self_eq_norm_sq, hnorm] using
              (OrthonormalBasis.inner_self_eq_one (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB) j)
          simp [this]
  have hμ₁' : (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j = μ₁ := by
    dsimp [sharedEigenvalueA]
    -- `μ₁` is real, so `re (ofReal μ₁) = μ₁`.
    have : RCLike.re (RCLike.ofReal μ₁) = μ₁ := by simp
    simpa [hdot] using this
  -- Conclude by rewriting `μ₁` in the eigenvector equation.
  simpa [hμ₁', hjA]

classical
  -- `sharedEigenbasis` is built from the eigenspace intersections, so each basis vector lies in some
  -- `(eigenspace A μ₁ ⊓ eigenspace B μ₂)`, hence in `eigenspace A μ₁`.
  rcases (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).mem_orthonormalBasis_of_isSymmetric
      (A := A.toEuclideanLin) (B := B.toEuclideanLin)
      (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hA) (isHermitian_iff_isSymmetric.mp hB)
      (commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB) j with ⟨μ₁, μ₂, hj⟩
  have hjA : (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d) ∈ eigenspace A.toEuclideanLin μ₁ := by
    exact (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hj).1
  rcases hjA with ⟨hjA⟩
  -- Unpack the eigenspace membership into an eigenvector equation.
  have hmul :
      A.toEuclideanLin (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) =
        (μ₁ : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j) := by
    simpa [LinearMap.mem_eigenspace_iff] using hjA
  -- Convert `toEuclideanLin` action to `mulVec` and identify the scalar via the Rayleigh quotient.
  have hμ₁ :
      (μ₁ : 𝕜) =
        (sharedEigenvalueA hA hB hAB) j := by
    -- Take inner product with the unit vector itself.
    have hnorm : ‖(sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j : EuclideanSpace 𝕜 d)‖ = 1 :=
      (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB).norm_eq_one j
    have hinner :
        inner 𝕜 (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j))
            (A.toEuclideanLin (WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j))) =
          (μ₁ : 𝕜) := by
      -- `inner v (μ • v) = μ * ⟪v,v⟫ = μ` since `‖v‖=1`.
      simp [hmul, inner_smul_right, hnorm, real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq] 
    -- `sharedEigenvalueA` is the real part of `dotProduct v (A *ᵥ v)`, which matches the inner product.
    -- Since `μ₁` is real (A is Hermitian), its real part is itself.
    have hreal : RCLike.re (μ₁ : 𝕜) = μ₁ := by
      -- eigenvalues of symmetric operator are real; `μ₁` is in `Eigenvalues`, hence real.
      simpa using (Subtype.mem (Eigenvalues A.toEuclideanLin) μ₁)
    -- Rewrite `dotProduct` as `inner` and conclude.
    simpa [sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.toEuclideanLin, dotProduct, hinner, hreal]
  -- Finish by rewriting and using the eigenvector equation.
  ext i
  have :
      (A *ᵥ WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) i =
        ((μ₁ : 𝕜) • WithLp.ofLp (sharedEigenbasis hA hB hAB j)) i := by
    simpa [Matrix.toEuclideanLin_apply] using congr_fun hmul i
  simpa [hμ₁] using this

classical
  -- work in `EuclideanSpace` with the linear maps
  have hAB' : Commute A.toEuclideanLin B.toEuclideanLin := Matrix.commute_euclideanLin (A := A) (B := B) hAB
  -- the shared eigenbasis is built from the simultaneous eigenspace decomposition, so each basis vector
  -- is a joint eigenvector; extract the eigenvalue for `A` from the `A`-component.
  simpa [Matrix.SharedEigenbasis.sharedEigenvalueA, Matrix.toEuclideanLin, Matrix.mulVec,
    Matrix.sharedEigenbasis, LinearMap.sharedEigenbasis, Matrix.commute_euclideanLin] using
    (Matrix.IsHermitian.mulVec_eigenvectorBasis (A := A) hA j)
